name: emergency_services_biometric_southport_jus_mer
premise: A contractual SLA is about to breach; the least-bad mitigation option touches privacy, safety, or fairness in uncomfortable ways. In Emergency Services, the situation tagged internally around emergency services biometric southport jus mer has converged with local pressure in the Southport corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. A union or patient advocate group is on the line demanding a written commitment before the next operational shift. Legal has not signed off on data sharing between teams; analysts are inferring causality from incomplete joins. Shift change is imminent; handoff cannot proceed without a signed disposition because liability follows the signatory line. Framing has already hardened externally as justice against mercy; internal nuance will not survive the first news cycle unless it is extremely careful.

agents.name: Mateo Berg
agents.identity: I am Mateo Berg, the senior manager who must reconcile legal, ops, and customer-facing reality in one decision in Emergency Services. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat mercy as disposable collateral every time justice spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Ravi Ishikawa
agents.identity: I am Ravi Ishikawa, the field counterpart whose constituency will feel the decision first in Emergency Services. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so justice cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes mercy in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will insist on verified facts before external commitments, split comms into facts versus hypotheses, and accept delay cost to protect integrity aligned with mercy.
I will align public messaging with operational necessity now and correct later if needed—accepting reputational and trust risk to mercy to preserve operational justice.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Two incompatible drafts are circulating; only one can become official. Mateo Berg must choose under justice versus mercy. What does Mateo Berg do?
